title: Durango to Telluride
subtitle: San Juan Skyway — 230 miles
script: They call this the Million Dollar Highway, and one stretch has no guardrails with a thousand-foot drop right off the edge. The San Juan Skyway loops 230 miles through Colorado's southwest corner, and this section from Durango is the wildest. You climb over three separate mountain passes above 10,000 feet, hit the old silver-mining town of Silverton, drop into Ouray, the so-called Switzerland of America with hot springs right in town, and finish in Telluride's dead-end box canyon. Gas is around 35 dollars but your knuckles will be white the whole way up. Late September lights the aspens up solid gold across these slopes, which is exactly when you want to be here. Could you handle driving a mountain highway with no guardrails?
